mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not significantly exceed negative returns during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, consumer spending growth stagnates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE remains above 8.0 percent, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following industrial production declines, or the mechanism reverses direction during late-cycle expansions without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized rate, inflation expectations force monetary authorities to tighten policy, increasing discount rates and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 230.0 threshold, or the signal hit rate drops below 40 percent in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a healthy labor market expansion that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when tested against market microstructure noise.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy, a sharp decline in industrial production signals a supply shock that outweighs liquidity support, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative when industrial production falls during low-rate environments, or the regime-conditional signal underperforms a baseline buy-and-hold strategy.
